{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "lib": ["dom", "dom.iterable", "esnext"],
    "allowJs": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "strict": true,
    "noEmit": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "module": "esnext",
    "moduleResolution": "bundler",
    "resolveJsonModule": true,
    "isolatedModules": true,
    "jsx": "preserve",
    "incremental": true,
    "plugins": [{ "name": "next" }],
    "paths": {
      "@/*": ["./src/*"]
    }
  },
  "include": ["next-env.d.ts", "**/*.ts", "**/*.tsx", ".next/types/**/*.ts"],
  "exclude": ["node_modules"]
}

/node_modules
/.next

next-env.d.ts

# Taran Khan — Writer & Journalist A minimal, editorial portfolio for author Taran Khan. Built with Next.js 15 App Router, React 19, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS. ## Stack | Layer | Choice | |-------|--------| | Framework | Next.js 15 (App Router) | | Language | TypeScript | | Styling | Tailwind CSS | | Display Font | Fraunces (Google Fonts) | | Body Font | Source Serif 4 (Google Fonts) | | UI Font | IBM Plex Sans (Google Fonts) | ## Palette - **Stone Paper** `#F2F0EB` - **Dusk Indigo** `#4A5D7A` - **Near Black** `#171412` ## Structure ``` src/ ├── app/ │ ├── layout.tsx # Root layout + fonts + metadata │ ├── globals.css # Tailwind + custom utilities │ ├── page.tsx # Home / About │ ├── book/ │ │ └── page.tsx # Shadow City │ ├── reportage/ │ │ └── page.tsx # Journalism index │ ├── workshops/ │ │ └── page.tsx # Teaching + consultations │ ├── events/ │ │ └── page.tsx # Talks + podcasts │ └── contact/ │ └── page.tsx # Email + newsletter ├── components/ │ ├── Navigation.tsx # Sticky header + mobile menu │ └── Footer.tsx # Simple footer ├── data/ │ └── content.ts # All site content (copy) └── lib/ └── utils.ts # CN utility ``` ## Setup 1. **Install dependencies** ```bash npm install ``` 2. **Run locally** ```bash npm run dev ``` Open [http://localhost:3000](http://localhost:3000) 3. **Build for production** ```bash npm run build ``` ## Deploy to Vercel ### Option A — Git Push (Recommended) 1. Push this folder to a GitHub repository. 2. Go to [vercel.com/new](https://vercel.com/new) and import the repo. 3. Vercel auto-detects **Next.js**. Click **Deploy**. 4. Add your custom domain in Project Settings → Domains. ### Option B — Vercel CLI ```bash npm i -g vercel vercel ``` ## Post-Deploy Checklist - [ ] **Add images**: Place book cover and author photos in `public/images/` and replace the placeholder `
` elements in `src/app/book/page.tsx` with `` or `` tags. - [ ] **Connect forms**: Replace the two instances of `YOUR_FORM_ID` in `src/app/workshops/page.tsx` and `src/app/contact/page.tsx` with your actual Formspree form endpoint. - [ ] **Update links**: Replace all placeholder `#` URLs in `src/data/content.ts` with real article / retailer links. - [ ] **SEO**: Update default metadata in `src/app/layout.tsx` if needed. Page-specific titles and descriptions live in each `page.tsx`. ## Notes - All content is centralized in `src/data/content.ts`. Editing copy there updates the entire site. - The site has no external runtime dependencies beyond React/Next, keeping it fast and easy to maintain. - Typography is optimized via `next/font/google` for zero layout shift and no external font requests at render time.

import type { Metadata } from "next"; import Link from "next/link"; import { about } from "@/data/content"; export const metadata: Metadata = { title: "Taran Khan | Writer & Journalist — Author of Shadow City", description: "Taran Khan is an award-winning writer and journalist, author of Shadow City: A Woman Walks Kabul. Reportage and essays on Afghanistan, exile, and the life of cities.", }; export default function HomePage() { return ( <>

{about.headline}

); }
